title: penstemon crandallii var. crandallii
intro: Penstemon crandallii var. crandallii, also known as Crandall's penstemon, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Crandall's penstemon have been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Crandall's penstemon can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
